text: Deep learning techniques such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformers have been widely used in remote sensing change detection (CD). However, existing CD methods have their own limitations. For example, the limited receptive field of the deep CNNs inhibits them from learning global spatial information, while the computational cost of the Transformers limits their applications on high-resolution remote sensing images. In this paper, we investigate the State Space Model-based Mamba model for CD, which has shown the state-of-the-art performance in the natural language processing task. Three architectures named MambaBCD, MambaSCD and MambaBDA are then proposed for performing the binary change detection, semantic change detection and building damage assessment tasks, respectively. We name the Mamba-based encoder as Visual Mamba, which can learn the global spatial information for CD. We adopt three spatio-temporal modeling approaches named as the Attention-based Model, the Spatial Transformation Model and the Mamba-based Model to fully explore the advantages of the Mamba model for the task of CD. The experimental results on five datasets demonstrate that the proposed MambaCD frameworks outperform state-of-the-art CD methods using CNNs and Transformers, and they can be trained without using any trick training techniques. The results also show that the proposed MambaCD frameworks are more robust to the deteriorated remote sensing images. The code of the proposed MambaCD frameworks is available at https://github.com/ChenHongruixuan/MambaCD.
